title: Should check the TTL constraints in `create temporary table like ...`
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
create table t(t timestamp) ttl=`t` + interval 1 day;
create temporary table temp like t;
show create table temp;
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

temporary table should not have TTL options

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
> show create table temp;
+-------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Table | Create Table                                                                                                                                                       |
+-------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| temp  | CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE `temp` (                                                                                                                                    |
|       |   `t` timestamp NULL DEFAULT NULL                                                                                                                                  |
|       | ) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin /*T![ttl] TTL=`t` + INTERVAL 1 DAY */ /*T![ttl] TTL_ENABLE='ON' */ /*T![ttl] TTL_JOB_INTERVAL='24h' */ |
+-------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

master
poc: create table t(t timestamp) ttl=`t` + interval 1 day;
create temporary table temp like t;
key_bug_features: Temporary table inherits TTL from original table
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(id INT PRIMARY KEY, created_at TIMESTAMP) TTL=`created_at` + INTERVAL 2 DAY;
CREATE TABLE t2(id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp1 LIKE t1;
